author: nflaig
content:
lodestar does the same as teku, are you thinking about a similar note as in the https://ethereum.github.io/beacon-APIs/?urls.primaryName=dev#/Beacon/submitPoolAttestationsV2 description?

worth nothing there is already a 400 status code and the note here kinda suggests to apply gossip validation?
> A success response (20x) indicates that the bid passed gossip validation and was successfully broadcast onto the network.

> other clients
is there any other client besides prysm that is crazy enough to publish unvalidated messages? I thought it was just prysm that is doing this

author: mehdi.aouadi
content:
> lodestar does the same as teku, are you thinking about a similar note as in the https://ethereum.github.io/beacon-APIs/?urls.primaryName=dev#/Beacon/submitPoolAttestationsV2 description?
Based on this report: https://panda-uploads-production.devops-539.workers.dev/panda/uploads/90b447/teku-bid-rejection-report.html it looks like only Teku were rejecting bids that do not have matching proposer preferences. Internally we queue those for future processing and we retry them once we have new proposer preferences came in but that's an effort that is not required by the spec. Based on the spec https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/17016b664f92398c828f011900e8de050836cb55/specs/gloas/p2p-interface.md?plain=1#L420 those should be IGNORED. So the question would be: Should IGNORED bids be considered failed gossip validation and hence respond with a 400?

> is there any other client besides prysm that is crazy enough to publish unvalidated messages? I thought it was just prysm that is doing this
It looks like only Teku is rejecting those 😅

author: mehdi.aouadi
content: Important note: It could be a bug in Teku which is,for some reasons, not seeing the matching proposer preferences (not easy to figure out). But I would like to check how other clients behave first

author: nflaig
content: it's probably that, we are definitely validating them

author: nflaig
content:
> Internally we queue those for future processing and we retry
we are not doing this, and probably won't, but this is only on gossip right?

author: mehdi.aouadi
content: We do it for those submitted through the rest api too but I might reconsider that choice actually

author: nflaig
content: the proposer preferences should be available way ahead, so I am thinking it's probably not needed

author: nflaig
content:
I was thinking there was a note in the spec, but it's not for bids, it's for proposer preferences
> - _[IGNORE]_ The block with root `preferences.dependent_root` has been seen (via
>   gossip or non-gossip sources) (a client MAY queue the message for
>   re-processing once the block is retrieved).

but we aren't doing that either

author: mehdi.aouadi
content: makes sens for the proposer preferences (even though if has been triggered and is showing that's we're missing them for some reason). We do it when the parent block has not been seen yet too for example...

author: nflaig
content: I was talking with James about this and we concluded at some point that we shouldn't broadcast them right at slot 0 of epoch N, for epoch N+1

author: nflaig
content: lodestar just broadcast them 8 slots ahead or so

author: nflaig
content:
> Based on the spec https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/17016b664f92398c828f011900e8de050836cb55/specs/gloas/p2p-interface.md?plain=1#L420 those should be IGNORED. So the question would be: Should IGNORED bids be considered failed gossip validation and hence respond with a 400?
that seems right to me to respond with 400 in that case, we might return a 500 in lodestar, but that's sloppy error handling, outcome is the same though, we do not broadcast such bids to the network

author: 0xunclebill
content: we run full gossip validation as well

author: 0xunclebill
content: we'll send a bid to our reprocess queue if it arrives early

author: nflaig
content: you return 400 on reject and ignore cases?

author: 0xunclebill
content: yeah we'll return a 400 in both of those cases

author: nflaig
content: ok had codex fix this on our end too now, 500 seems wrong https://github.com/ChainSafe/lodestar/pull/9699

author: nflaig
content: but that seems like maybe 2 bugs here? so why does buildoor create bids if it hasn't seen proposer preferences, or maybe teku doesn't cache them properly?

author: nflaig
content: (assuming buildoor is just connected to the respective beacon node it publishes to)

author: mehdi.aouadi
content: yeah normally buildoor is connected to the BN. I’m leaning toward a Teku bug even though based on the logs we should be caching the proposer preferences. I’ll dig more into it more
